title: Features Data
description: Schema for the features.json data file that drives README feature grids and CLI reference tables.

version.description: Version of the project this data applies to (semver string).

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 date when this file was last updated (YYYY-MM-DD).

artifactTypes.description: Enumeration of artifact types the project manages.

Category.title: Feature Category
Category.description: A named group of related features shown as a section in the README.

name.description: Human-readable category name shown as a heading.

icon.description: Icon identifier (e.g. Lucide icon name) used by the web UI.

tagline.description: Short italic subtitle shown beneath the category heading.

features.description: List of features belonging to this category.

Feature.title: Feature
Feature.description: An individual feature entry displayed in the feature grid and/or CLI reference.

description.description: Full description of the feature (one to two sentences).

shortDescription.description: Compact description used in table cells (a few words).

since.description: The project version in which this feature was first introduced (semver).

webPage.description: Relative web UI route that exposes this feature (e.g. '/collection').

screenshot.description: Filename of the associated screenshot (matched against screenshots.json).

highlight.description: When true the feature name is rendered in bold in the feature grid.

ArtifactType.title: Artifact Type
ArtifactType.description: A type of artifact the project can manage.

description.description: Short description of the artifact type.

icon.description: Icon identifier used by the web UI.

color.description: Theme color token or CSS color name used for badges.

Stats.title: Project Stats
Stats.description: Aggregate numeric statistics shown in the feature section header.

webPages.description: Number of distinct web UI pages.

apiEndpoints.description: Total number of REST API endpoints.

modals.description: Number of distinct modal dialogs in the web UI.
